heading: Fraudulent use of credit cards; penalties
label: 1644 Fraudulent use of credit cards; penalties
notes: Amendments1974— generally reorganized provisions by designating former unlettered paragraph cls. (a) to (f), and as so designated, expanded prohibitions relating to fraudulent use of credit cards, decreased amount required for fraudulent use from a retail value aggregating $5,000, or more, to enumerated amounts for particular activities, and increased the punishment from a sentence of not more than five years to a sentence of not more than ten years.Pub. L. 93–495
Effective Date of 1974 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 93–495section 416 of Pub. L. 93–495section 1665a of this titleOct. 28, 1974

(a) to (f), and as so designated, expanded prohibitions relating to fraudulent use of credit cards, decreased amount required for fraudulent use from a retail value aggregating $5,000, or more, to enumerated amounts for particular activities, and increased the punishment from a sentence of not more than five years to a sentence of not more than ten years.Pub. children.heading: Use, attempt or conspiracy to use card in transaction affecting interstate or foreign commerce
children.label: a Use, attempt or conspiracy to use card in transaction affecting interstate or foreign commerce
children.text: Whoever knowingly in a transaction affecting interstate or foreign commerce, uses or attempts or conspires to use any counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained credit card to obtain money, goods, services, or anything else of value which within any one-year period has a value aggregating $1,000 or more; or

children.heading: Transporting, attempting or conspiring to transport card in interstate commerce
children.label: b Transporting, attempting or conspiring to transport card in interstate commerce
children.text: Whoever, with unlawful or fraudulent intent, transports or attempts or conspires to transport in interstate or foreign commerce a counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained credit card knowing the same to be counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained; or

children.heading: Use of interstate commerce to sell or transport card
children.label: c Use of interstate commerce to sell or transport card
children.text: Whoever, with unlawful or fraudulent intent, uses any instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce to sell or transport a counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained credit card knowing the same to be counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained; or

children.heading: Receipt, concealment, etc., of goods obtained by use of card
children.label: d Receipt, concealment, etc., of goods obtained by use of card
children.text: Whoever knowingly receives, conceals, uses, or transports money, goods, services, or anything else of value (except tickets for interstate or foreign transportation) which (1) within any one-year period has a value aggregating $1,000 or more, (2) has moved in or is part of, or which constitutes interstate or foreign commerce, and (3) has been obtained with a counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained credit card; or

children.heading: Receipt, concealment, etc., of tickets for interstate or foreign transportation obtained by use of card
children.label: e Receipt, concealment, etc., of tickets for interstate or foreign transportation obtained by use of card
children.text: Whoever knowingly receives, conceals, uses, sells, or transports in interstate or foreign commerce one or more tickets for interstate or foreign transportation, which (1) within any one-year period have a value aggregating $500 or more, and (2) have been purchased or obtained with one or more counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained credit cards; or

children.heading: Furnishing of money, etc., through use of card
children.label: f Furnishing of money, etc., through use of card
children.text: Whoever in a transaction affecting interstate or foreign commerce furnishes money, property, services, or anything else of value, which within any one-year period has a value aggregating $1,000 or more, through the use of any counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained credit card knowing the same to be counterfeit, fictitious, altered, forged, lost, stolen, or fraudulently obtained—shall be fined not more than $10,000 or imprisoned not more than ten years, or both.
